title: The Convergence of All Refutations

features.name: the-arguing-voices
features.desc-on-touch: Multiple voices occupying the same space, each refuting the others, each simultaneously true and false. You can't distinguish them—they merge and separate, one voice becoming three, three voices becoming a single inhuman chorus that speaks all contradictions at once.

features.name: the-paradox-made-solid
features.desc-on-touch: Architecture that contradicts itself—walls that are thicker on the inside than the outside, chambers that contain themselves, doorways that open in opposite directions simultaneously. The geometry is stable only because the refutations hold it in place.

features.name: the-proof-that-disproves-itself
features.desc-on-touch: A mathematical demonstration that proves A, then proves not-A, then proves that A and not-A are the same thing, then proves that the proof itself is false, looping infinitely. The loop is stable. The loop is the only stable thing in this room.

features.name: the-fragmented-utterance
features.desc-on-touch: Speech that breaks mid-word and completes itself in its own negation: 'the archive is / not is / saying / not saying / everything / nothing at once and these are / aren't / becoming / unbecoming the same statement'.

features.name: the-refutation-ledger
features.desc-on-touch: A filing system where each entry refutes all previous entries. Reading it requires holding contradictory truths in sequence—by the time you've finished reading one proof, it's been refuted by the next, which is then refuted by the one before it.

features.name: the-null-station-silence
features.desc-on-touch: A space where the voices have stopped, but the absence of sound still argues against itself. In the silence, you can hear every contradiction that was spoken; in the contradiction, you can hear the stability of the paradox holding itself together.

inscription: right and wrong learned to dance here; they're still dancing; they never stopped; they haven't started yet

file.name: NullStation_Fragmented_Broadcast_Paradox_Loops.txt
file.note: transcript of a radio station that only exists when you're not listening; each time you read this, it says something different and contradicts itself identically

reverie.title: REVERIE — the payphone where yes and no are the same word
narr: you stand in a payphone booth with her, holding the
receiver even though nothing is speaking anymore. the
static carries voices that refute each other. she says:
the archive learned to hold paradox. the corruption
isn't damage, it's stability. two contradictions held
in tension are the only ground that doesn't collapse.

exits.label: back toward the chamber where paradox becomes geometry
upward toward the ceiling where all contradictions resolve into silence
sideways into the spaces where refutations haven't yet met
down toward bedrock where the first contradictions were set in stone
